findings.message: T002 is self-contradictory: it redefines `TypeKindDto` as a single `Struct` variant with `serde tag = "struct"`, but then says `type_kind_to_dto` must keep emitting the legacy `unit_struct` / `tuple_struct` / `plain_struct` JSON. It also asks `CatalogueToExtendedCrateCodec` to propagate typestate through `encode_unit_struct` / `encode_tuple_struct`, even though the rustdoc `StructKind` output has no typestate slot. As written, the codec behavior cannot be implemented consistently.

IN-04 says the signal evaluator must be updated alongside the linter, but the task graph only assigns T002/T003 and never creates a task for the evaluator-side `TypeKindV2` typestate matching. That leaves the `signal evaluator` part of the requirement uncovered, so the Blue acceptance criteria for unit/tuple typestate states cannot be reached by the planned work alone.

T004 tells implementers to migrate `domain-types.json`'s `TypeKindV2.kind` entry to a `kind: "struct"` schema, but the same plan's domain catalogue defines the new `TypeKindV2` as an enum with `Struct(StructKind)`, `Enum`, and `TypeAlias`. That `kind: "struct"` shape belongs to the DTO/wire representation described in T002, not to the domain type catalogue entry, so following T004 would corrupt the catalogue instead of migrating it.

AC-05 and AC-06 require malformed catalogue declarations to be rejected at parse time, but task coverage maps both only to T001, which is the domain schema change. The plan itself places DTO/decode parsing in T002 and tests in T005, so the current coverage lets the plan complete without assigning the parse-boundary rejection or its tests to any relevant task.

AC-03/AC-04 require the codec to attach a typestate marker when encoding `rustdoc_types::StructKind::Unit`/`Tuple`, but the implementation plan explicitly says rustdoc `StructKind` has no typestate slot and the marker must not be mixed into A-side `ExtendedCrate` encoding. T005 then covers these ACs with CatalogueDocument JSON round-trip tests instead. This makes the acceptance criteria and task plan contradictory: either AC-03/AC-04 should target the JSON/domain round-trip, or the plan must define where the marker is supplied and evaluated for rustdoc-derived shapes.

T004 makes the active catalogue migration a no-op, but T002 explicitly says the old `unit_struct` / `tuple_struct` / `plain_struct` wire formats will no longer be output and that T004 must migrate the active catalogues. The scoped active catalogues still contain old wire tags such as `plain_struct` and `unit_struct`, so leaving them “unchanged” makes the plan internally contradictory and likely leaves the catalogues incompatible once the new DTO lands.
